en: Aberration A departure from what is normal, or usual.
Deviation Abnormality
Oddity Owing to a strange mental aberration he forgot his own phone number.
Acumen The ability to make good judgments.
Intelligence Astuteness
Discernment During the board meeting, he demonstrated
considerable business acumen.
Aggrandize To make something more powerful or important.
Elevate Glorify
Promote A community shall not aggrandize itself at the expense of its neighbours.
Ambivalent Having two opposing feelings at the same
time. Undecided
Uncertain Wavering
The story was an interesting read, full of dark imagery and ambivalent characters.
Arbitrary Based on random choice or personal whim.
Inconsistent Aimless
Haphazard Although arbitrary arrests are illegal, they
are not allowed to occur in many societies. Barren
Not producing or unable to produce plants. Desolate
Infertile Unproductive
Because of heavy rainfall, that remote barren land has blossomed into rich granaries.
Beguile To entertain and convince by flattery.
Mislead Deceive
Hoodwink She was cunning enough to beguile her boyfriend
into doing the work for her. Convivial
Pleasant and friendly in manner or attitude. Good-humoured
Amiable Cordial
If you were more convivial to your employees, they would probably stop quitting their jobs.
Commensurate Corresponding in size or degree, in proportion.
Equivalent Matching
Correlative The salary and fringe benefits will be commensurate
with age and experience. Concomitant
Existing or occurring at the same time. Coincident
Coexisting Concurrent
Cultures that were better at trading saw a concomitant increase in their prosperity.
Callous Without sympathy or feeling for other people.
Unfeeling Inhumane
Merciless Despite her wealth, the old lady was a callous
woman who never gave to anyone in need. Camaraderie
Mutual trust and friendship among people. Fellowship
Brotherhood Solidarity
They have developed a real camaraderie after working together for so long.
Circumlocution Complicated way of expressing something.
Ambiguity Vagueness
Obscurity The lawyer made use of circumlocution to confuse
the jury into considering his client's innocence.
Death A scarcity or lack of something.
Deficiency Paucity
Scarcity Because there was a dearth of evidence, the
district attorney had to drop the charges. Delicacy
Something especially rare or expensive. Elegance
Dignity Majesty
These objects are very old and should be treated with great delicacy.
Debunk To show that an idea or belief is false.
Disprove Challenge
Negate The article published in the newspaper debunks
the notion that life exists on Mars. Dogmatic
To follow a set of rules in an arrogant manner. Doctrinaire
Adamant Emphatic
He refused to listen to others, so everyone considered the politician to be too dogmatic.
Ephemeral Something fleeting or short-lived.
Temporary Fleeting
Transitory The loud thunderstorm was ephemeral, starting
suddenly and gone within seconds.
Empirical Based on observation or practical experience.
Pragmatic Experimental
Factual This book attempts to present a large mass
of empirical evidence to support the claim. Endeavour
An effort or attempt to do something. Attempt
Undertaking Exercise
In recent times there have been great advances in the field of scientific endeavour.
Enormity Something extreme almost beyond comprehension.
Hugeness Immensity
Dreadfulness The enormity of the racial crime has shocked
even experienced policemen. Exacerbate
To make something that is already bad worse. Aggravate
Worsen Infuriate
Their angry comments have exacerbated tensions in the negotiation process.
Fathom Understand something complicated or mysterious.
Comprehend Understand
Discern I couldn't fathom their reasons for making
such a foolish decision. Homogeneous
Of the same or a similar kind or nature. Identical
Analogous Congruent
We prefer working and socializing with a homogeneous group of people.
Hubris Excessive pride or self-confidence
Pompousness Arrogance
Haughtiness It is thought that hubris leads to the downfall
of many celebrities and politicians. Instigate
To cause something to happen or begin. Provoke
Stimulate Arouse
The administration will instigate new measures to combat drug trafficking.
Iconoclast Someone who opposes society's beliefs or customs.
Dissident Nonconformist
Bohemian The successful entrepreneur is an iconoclast
who is not afraid to take risks. Idiosyncratic
Having strange or unusual way of behaving. Peculiar
Eccentric Outlandish
It is difficult to evaluate different cultures because they are traditionally idiosyncratic.
Inundate To cover an area with a large amount of water.
Submerge Overflow
Overwhelm Our neighborhood is being inundated by the rising waters of the Colorado River.
Laconic Using few words in speech or writing.
Reticent Reserved
Elliptical The writer was laconic when drafting the poem and used very few words in each line.
Leverage Use something to maximum advantage.
Support Advantage
Dominance If the United Nations had more troops in the area, it would have greater leverage.
Mandacious One who tells lies habitually and intentionally.
Fraudulent Dishonest
Untruthful Some of these statements are misleading and some are downright mendacious.
Mundane Very ordinary and therefore not interesting.
Ordinary Commonplace
Humdrum The science experiment would be more interesting if the materials weren't so mundane.
Obdurate Stubbornly persistent in wrongdoing.
Obstinate Stubborn
Unyielding Despite the defendant's apology, the judge was obdurate and gave him a 20-year sentence.
Panacea A solution for all problems or difficulties.
Remedy Magic formula
Cure Unfortunately, there is no panacea that will make cancer abolished immediately.
Paramount Very important of highest rank or importance.
Predominant Leading
Foremost Personal responsibility and accountability are paramount for this type of job.
Paradigm A distinct set of concepts or thought patterns.
Model Archetype
Prototype After the terrorist attack, the government created a new paradigm for domestic security.
Plethora A large or excessive amount of something.
Profusion Plenty
Myriad Since the recent elections, there has been
a plethora of anti-government sentiment.
Phenomenon An extraordinary occurrence or situation.
Happening Circumstance
Incident There's evidence to suggest that child abuse is not just a recent phenomenon.
Promulgate To announce new ideas and beliefs publicly.
Broadcast Proclaim
Disseminate The actor makes use of social media to promulgate the release of his film projects.
Reminiscent Awakening memories of something similar.
Evocative Suggestive
Nostalgic This tale is perhaps reminiscent of human sacrifice amongst the Greeks.
Innocence Sagacity
Good judgment based on practical knowledge.
Wisdom Intelligence
Learning Because of their professor's sagacity in teaching,
his students had no problem passing the exam.
Serendipity A seeming gift for finding something good.
Chance Fortune
Coincidence It had to be serendipity when we found our
Surreptitious Done secretly, without anyone seeing or knowing.
Clandestine Stealthy
Secret Files and tapes that were previously surreptitious
have now been leaked to the public.
Taboo Prescribed by society as improper or unacceptable.
Forbidden Prohibited
Banned Questions and problems that were once taboo are now discussed openly.
Travesty
An absurd or grotesque misrepresentation.
Mockery Distortion
Falsification
Not allowing her to speak in her own defense was a travesty of justice.
Ubiquitous
Present, appearing, or found everywhere.
Worldwide Prevalent
Widespread
Because of the internet, the company's name has become ubiquitous all over the world.
Unanimous
Having the agreement and consent of all.
Harmonious Congenial
Like-minded
The unanimous consent of all the shareholders was required to approve the proposal.
Vigilant
Keeping careful watch for possible danger.
Watchful Circumspect
Observant
The conservation organization is ever vigilant for threats to the habitats of birds.
Whimsical
Acting or behaving in a playful manner.
Fanciful Imaginative
Humorous
The author turned out to be as whimsical as the magical characters in his children's book.
